title: salsola subaphylla subsp. arenaria
intro: Salsola subaphylla subsp. arenaria, also known as Prickly Saltwort, is a type of succulent shrub that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. Traditionally, Prickly Saltwort has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat conditions such as hypertension, rheumatism, and gout due to its high concentration of alkaloids and other active compounds. The health benefits of Prickly Saltwort include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural anti-oxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Prickly Saltwort is often dried and made into teas, capsules, or infusions, and can also be used topically in the form of creams or ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
